text.title: Cron Automation for VPS Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Cron Automation for VPS` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every cron script MUST start with `set -euo pipefail`. Missing strict mode = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Without strict mode, a failed pipe in step 3 silently continues; script exits 0 despite broken state.

rule.statement: Jobs > 1 minute MUST run inside `flock -n /var/lock/<job>.lock`. Missing flock = REJECT for long jobs.
rule.rationale: Hourly cron can fire while previous run still executes; two backups racing = corrupted dump.

rule.statement: Every cron entry MUST export PATH + relevant env vars at the top of the entry or via SHELL=/bin/bash + a wrapper script. Implicit env = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Cron runs under sh with PATH=/usr/bin:/bin; tools at /usr/local/bin or user-installed (mise, nvm) are missing.

rule.statement: Every cron job MUST write a per-run log file (rotated daily) AND emit a single-line summary on completion. Logs-only OR summary-only = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Logs let you debug; summaries let the operator notice without reading every line. Both are required for self-driving infra.

rule.statement: Failure alerts MUST fire once per failed run via Telegram/webhook. Success messages MUST be silent (FLOW style). Inverted = REJECT.
rule.rationale: If success spams the channel, real failures get lost in the noise. Silent-on-success forces attention to failures.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
